efficacyPairs.questionText: Move one letter from one word to the other to make two new words: WHEAT  EAR
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: Look at **BLAND** and **OAR**. Try this: cover up the first letter of BLAND with your finger. Can you read a real word underneath? If YES, you might have found the answer already!
lessonLearningGoal: A brilliant shortcut: the FIRST letter of word 1 is often the one that moves!
How to quickly test this by covering the front letter — it takes just seconds

efficacyPairs.questionText: Move one letter from one word to the other to make two new words: BAND CAR
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: Look at **PLANT** and **OUR**. Put your finger over the last letter of PLANT. Can you read a real word? If YES, test inserting that letter into word 2.

efficacyPairs.questionText: Move one letter from one word to the other to make two new words: BAND BAR
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: Look at **GRAVE** and **OAR**. When you remove a middle letter from GRAVE, the letters on EITHER SIDE close up. They stay in order!
lessonLearningGoal: The trickier cases: sometimes the letter hiding in the MIDDLE is the one that moves!
Why trying first and last before the middle keeps you fast and organised
